div.p: Call stack from a + b through __add__ to Value creation and _backward closure capture
python a = Value(2.0) b = Value(3.0) c = a + b # ← Triggers add

ul.li: c._backward (gradient function) Backward traversal visits nodes in reverse topological order, calling each _backward to propagate gradients.

div.p: python def add(self, other): # self = a (Value with data=2.0) # other = b (Value with data=3.0)
other = other if isinstance(other, Value) else Value(other)
div.h1: other is already Value(3.0) → no conversion

div.p: python def _backward(): # Captures: self, other, out self.grad += out.grad # a.grad += c.grad other.grad += out.grad # b.grad += c.grad out._backward = _backward
python return out # Value(5.0) with graph connectivity

ul.li: out → reference to result c (Value 5.0) Function Body: python def _backward(): self.grad += out.grad other.grad += out.grad

div.p: c.grad = 1.0 │ ▼ ┌─────────────────────┐ │ _backward() │ │ │ │ a.grad += 1.0 │ → a.grad = 1.0 │ b.grad += 1.0 │ → b.grad = 1.0 └─────────────────────┘ Result: Both inputs receive gradient 1 (correct for addition: d(a+b)/da = 1, d(a+b)/db = 1)
